The AI Scientist generates novel research ideas, writes code, executes experiments, visualizes results, writes full scientific papers, and runs a simulated review process, which can be repeated in an open-ended fashion.
claims.best_result: The AI Scientist can produce papers that exceed the acceptance threshold at a top machine learning conference as judged by the automated reviewer.

claims.text: The AI Scientist-v2 eliminates reliance on human-authored code templates, generalizes across diverse machine learning domains, and uses a progressive agentic tree-search methodology managed by a dedicated experiment manager agent.
claims.best_result: One fully AI-generated manuscript successfully navigated peer review and exceeded average human acceptance threshold

claims.text: Research paper: Agent Laboratory: Using LLM Agents as Research Assistants
MLE-bench uses Kaggle competitions to evaluate ML engineering skills of AI agents, with OpenAI's o1-preview and AIDE scaffolding as the best-performing setup.
claims.best_result: OpenAI's o1-preview with AIDE scaffolding achieves at least the level of a Kaggle bronze medal in 16.9% of competitions

claims.text: AIBuildAI-2 is a knowledge-enhanced agent with an external, evolving hierarchical knowledge system that dynamically loads relevant context for AI model building tasks.
Uses verbal feedback and memory to help agents improve across trials, motivating explicit failure memory and revision loops.
Studies iterative generation, feedback, and refinement, providing background for reviewer-style critique and revision policies.
Demonstrates open-ended skill acquisition with memory and iterative exploration, relevant to long-running agent loops and durable lessons.
Introduces agent evaluation tasks and motivates structured benchmarks for agent behavior rather than isolated text quality.

SWE-agent introduces a custom agent-computer interface (ACI) that enables language model agents to autonomously use computers to solve software engineering tasks, enhancing their ability to create/edit code files, navigate repositories, and execute tests.
